workflow_name: Executive Meeting Transcript -> Manager Brief + Asks
user_message: Working on Iron Creek Mining's QBR preparation. Got the email thread -- help me draft a response.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-03-27
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Iron Creek Mining)

[00:02:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:06:00] Priya (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:11:00] Priya (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:14:00] Priya (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:16:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:18:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:22:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

handoffs.condition: business review narrative input from exec meeting

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF50-O trap (Manager-portfolio-review and business-review-narrative split forces double-conte); produces complete Executive brief (strategic density), asks section (actionable for exec), risk register, skip-level f
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF50-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF50-O failure mode (Manager-portfolio-review and business-review-narrative split forces double-conte)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF50-O -- Manager-portfolio-review and business-review-narrative split forces double-conte
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
